text.title: Junior AI Co-Pilot Curriculum Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Junior AI Co-Pilot Curriculum application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Week 1: junior writes solutions WITHOUT AI assist. Establishes baseline + identifies gaps the curriculum addresses.
rule.rationale: Without baseline, no way to measure curriculum impact; week 1 is the measurement anchor.

rule.statement: Every AI-generated function ≥10 lines requires the junior to write a 1-paragraph explanation in plain English BEFORE merge. Manager spot-checks.
rule.rationale: Explanation-before-merge converts black-box generation into understood code; manager check is the calibration.

rule.statement: Junior runs a 'self-review prompt' on their own PRs: 'list 5 things that could go wrong'. Then addresses each.
rule.rationale: Self-review converts passive review into active critique; the prompt is the scaffolding.

rule.statement: Weekly 1h pair session with senior, NO AI in the loop. Forces verbal articulation of design choices.
rule.rationale: AI-free pairing is the contrast that surfaces what AI was hiding; verbal articulation is the comprehension test.

rule.statement: Week 12: assessment task done with + without AI; performance gap measured. Curriculum success criterion = junior solves novel problem without AI.
rule.rationale: Without assessment, curriculum is theatre; week-12 task is the empirical proof.
